slides.title: The RTL Philosophy
bulletPoints: React Testing Library (RTL) is built on a very simple premise:
'The more your tests resemble the way your software is used, the more confidence they can give you.'
RTL encourages you not to test implementation details like state values or internal instance methods.
slides.note: RTL renders components into a virtual DOM via jsdom, avoiding the overhead of a real browser while still behaving like one.

slides.title: Rendering Components
bulletPoints: The 'render' function from '@testing-library/react' is used to mount your component.
It attaches the component to document.body and provides cleanup utilities.
Combine this with test runners like Vitest or Jest.
slides.note: Always use 'screen' rather than destructuring queries from 'render'. 'screen' lets you query any element in document.body globally.
